get.summary: Lists storage gateways in the specified organization.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Optional. The maximum number of storage gateways to return in the
response. The server can return fewer storage gateways than requested.
If unspecified, the server picks an appropriate default.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Optional. A pagination token returned from a previous call to
`ListStorageGateways` that indicates from where listing should continue.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Optional. An expression for filtering the results of the request.
Filter rules are case insensitive. Filterable fields:

+ `state`
+ `displayName`
+ `createTime` (supports comparison operators)

Examples:

+ `state = ACTIVE` — only active storage gateways.
+ `displayName = "My Gateway"` — exact match on display name.
+ `createTime > "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"` — created after date.

For more information, see [AIP-160](https://aip.dev/160).

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Optional. A comma-separated list of fields to order by. The default
order is ascending. Use "desc" after a field name for descending.
Supported fields:

+ `displayName`
+ `createTime`
+ `name`

Example: `createTime desc, displayName`

If not specified, the results are ordered by `name` ascending.

post.summary: Creates a new storage gateway in the specified organization.
post.description:
The caller must have `storage.storageGateways.create` permission on the
parent organization.

parameters.name: storageGateway
parameters.description: Required. The storage gateway to create.

parameters.name: storageGatewayId
parameters.description:
Optional. A unique identifier for the storage gateway. If not provided,
the server will generate one.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description:
Optional. If set to true, the request will only validate the request
without persisting it.

get.summary:
Fetches a storage gateway resource identified by the specified resource
name.

delete.description:
The caller must have `storage.storageGateways.delete` permission on the
specified storage gateway.

parameters.name: etag
parameters.description:
Optional. The etag known to the client for the expected state of the
storage gateway. This is to be used for optimistic concurrency.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description:
Optional. If set to true, the request will only validate the request
without persisting it.

patch.summary:
Updates a storage gateway resource. Use the field mask to specify which
fields to update. Omitting the field mask updates all mutable fields.
patch.description:
The caller must have `storage.storageGateways.update` permission on the
specified storage gateway.

parameters.name: storageGateway
parameters.description:
Required. The new definition of the storage gateway. The `name` field
must be populated to identify the storage gateway to update.

displayName.description:
Required. A human-readable name for the storage gateway. Must be at
most 60 characters long.

state.description: Output only. The current lifecycle state of the storage gateway.

hostname.description:
Output only. The hostname assigned to this storage gateway, for example
`{name}.storage.pivox.app`.

ipAddresses.description:
Required. The IP addresses that the storage gateway listens on.
At least one IP address must be provided.

registrationToken.description:
Output only. The registration token used to register agents with this
storage gateway.

targetVersion.description: Optional. The desired software version for the storage gateway.

currentVersion.description:
Output only. The currently running software version of the storage
gateway.

cacheConfig.description: Optional. Cache configuration for the storage gateway.

certState.description: Output only. The current state of the TLS certificate.

certExpiryTime.description: Output only. The expiration time of the TLS certificate.

annotations.description:
Optional. Annotations associated with this storage gateway.
Annotations are key-value pairs that can be used to organize and track
resources.

etag.description:
Output only. A checksum computed by the server based on the current value
of the StorageGateway resource. This may be sent on update and delete
requests to ensure the client has an up-to-date value before proceeding.

creator.description:
Output only. The resource name of the user who created the storage
gateway.

updater.description:
Output only. The resource name of the user who last updated the storage
gateway.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the storage gateway was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the storage gateway was last modified.

schema.description:
Required. The new definition of the storage gateway. The `name` field
must be populated to identify the storage gateway to update.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description:
Optional. If set to true, the request will only validate the request
without persisting it.

get.summary:
Returns an install script for the specified storage gateway. The script
is customised with the provided configuration parameters.

parameters.name: cacheDir
parameters.description: Optional. The directory to use for caching.

parameters.name: cacheSizeGb
parameters.description: Optional. The cache size in gigabytes.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: Optional. The port the gateway agent should listen on (1-65535).

parameters.name: bindAddress
parameters.description: Optional. The IP address the gateway agent should bind to.

parameters.name: httpProxy
parameters.description: Optional. HTTP proxy URL for outbound connections.

parameters.name: httpsProxy
parameters.description: Optional. HTTPS proxy URL for outbound connections.

parameters.name: noProxy
parameters.description: Optional. Comma-separated list of hosts that should bypass the proxy.

parameters.name: telemetry
parameters.description: Optional. Whether to enable telemetry collection.

post.summary:
Rotates the registration token for a storage gateway. The previous token
is immediately invalidated and a new token is generated.

get.summary: Returns an uninstall script for the specified storage gateway.

post.summary:
Initiates an upgrade of the storage gateway to the specified target
version. The upgrade is performed as a rolling update across all agents.

CacheConfigEvictionPolicy.description:
The eviction policy used when the cache reaches its maximum size.

 - EVICTION_POLICY_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified eviction policy.
 - LRU: Least Recently Used: evicts the least recently accessed items first.
 - LFU: Least Frequently Used: evicts the least frequently accessed items first.

StorageGatewayCertState.description:
TLS certificate lifecycle states.

 - CERT_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified certificate state.
 - PENDING: The certificate is being provisioned.
 - CERT_ACTIVE: The certificate is active and valid.
 - EXPIRING: The certificate is approaching its expiration date.
 - EXPIRED: The certificate has expired.

StorageGatewaysRotateRegistrationTokenBo.description:
The request sent to the
[RotateRegistrationToken][pivox.storage.v1.StorageGateways.RotateRegistrationToken]
method.

targetVersion.description: Required. The target version to upgrade to.

StorageGatewaysUpgradeGatewayBody.description:
The request sent to the
[UpgradeGateway][pivox.storage.v1.StorageGateways.UpgradeGateway]
method.

name.description:
The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that
originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the
`name` should be a resource name ending with `operations/{unique_id}`.

metadata.description:
Service-specific metadata associated with the operation.  It typically
contains progress information and common metadata such as create time.
Some services might not provide such metadata.  Any method that returns a
long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

done.description:
If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress.
If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is
available.

error.description: The error result of the operation in case of failure or cancellation.

response.description:
The normal, successful response of the operation.  If the original
method returns no data on success, such as `Delete`, the response is
`google.protobuf.Empty`.  If the original method is standard
`Get`/`Create`/`Update`, the response should be the resource.  For other
methods, the response should have the type `XxxResponse`, where `Xxx`
is the original method name.  For example, if the original method name
is `TakeSnapshot()`, the inferred response type is
`TakeSnapshotResponse`.

longrunningOperation.description:
This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a
network API call.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

code.description:
The status code, which should be an enum value of
[google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description:
A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
[google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized
by the client.

details.description:
A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
message types for APIs to use.

rpcStatus.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

maxSizeGb.description: Optional. The maximum cache size in gigabytes. Must be positive.

evictionPolicy.description: Optional. The eviction policy to use when the cache is full.

ttlHours.description: Optional. The time-to-live for cached items, in hours. Must be positive.

v1CacheConfig.description: Cache configuration for a storage gateway.

v1GetInstallScriptResponse.description:
The response returned from the
[GetInstallScript][pivox.storage.v1.StorageGateways.GetInstallScript]
method.

v1GetUninstallScriptResponse.description:
The response returned from the
[GetUninstallScript][pivox.storage.v1.StorageGateways.GetUninstallScript]
method.

storageGateways.description: The list of storage gateways for the specified organization.

nextPageToken.description:
A pagination token to be used to retrieve the next page of results.
If empty, this response contains the last page of results.

v1ListStorageGatewaysResponse.description:
The response returned from the
[ListStorageGateways][pivox.storage.v1.StorageGateways.ListStorageGateways]
method.

name.title:
Output only. The resource name of the storage gateway. Format:
`organizations/{organization}/storageGateways/{storage_gateway}`

displayName.description:
Required. A human-readable name for the storage gateway. Must be at
most 60 characters long.

state.description: Output only. The current lifecycle state of the storage gateway.

hostname.description:
Output only. The hostname assigned to this storage gateway, for example
`{name}.storage.pivox.app`.

ipAddresses.description:
Required. The IP addresses that the storage gateway listens on.
At least one IP address must be provided.

registrationToken.description:
Output only. The registration token used to register agents with this
storage gateway.

targetVersion.description: Optional. The desired software version for the storage gateway.

currentVersion.description:
Output only. The currently running software version of the storage
gateway.

cacheConfig.description: Optional. Cache configuration for the storage gateway.

certState.description: Output only. The current state of the TLS certificate.

certExpiryTime.description: Output only. The expiration time of the TLS certificate.

annotations.description:
Optional. Annotations associated with this storage gateway.
Annotations are key-value pairs that can be used to organize and track
resources.

etag.description:
Output only. A checksum computed by the server based on the current value
of the StorageGateway resource. This may be sent on update and delete
requests to ensure the client has an up-to-date value before proceeding.

creator.description:
Output only. The resource name of the user who created the storage
gateway.

updater.description:
Output only. The resource name of the user who last updated the storage
gateway.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the storage gateway was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the storage gateway was last modified.

v1StorageGateway.description:
A storage gateway deployed within an organization. Storage gateways act
as caching proxies that accelerate access to remote storage backends.

v1StorageGatewayState.description:
Storage gateway lifecycle states.

 - STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state. This is only useful for distinguishing unset values.
 - PROVISIONING: The gateway is being provisioned and is not yet ready to serve traffic.
 - ACTIVE: The gateway is active and serving traffic normally.
 - DEGRADED: The gateway is operating in a degraded state. Some functionality may
be unavailable.
 - OFFLINE: The gateway is offline and not serving traffic.
